query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- Lesson 2: The Torah
(690002, 'What does "Torah" mean in Hebrew?', 'multiple_choice', '["History", "Law/Instruction", "Prophecy", "Wisdom"]', 'Law/Instruction', 1),
(690002, 'Which book of the Torah describes the creation of the world?', 'multiple_choice', '["Exodus", "Leviticus", "Genesis", "Deuteronomy"]', 'Genesis', 2),
(690002, 'The exodus from Egypt is primarily recorded in which book?', 'multiple_choice', '["Genesis", "Exodus", "Numbers", "Deuteronomy"]', 'Exodus', 3),
(690002, 'Which book contains detailed laws about sacrifices and priestly duties?', 'multiple_choice', '["Genesis", "Exodus", "Leviticus", "Numbers"]', 'Leviticus', 4),
(690002, 'The Ten Commandments were given at:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mount Carmel", "Mount Sinai", "Mount Zion", "Mount Nebo"]', 'Mount Sinai', 5),
(690002, 'Who is traditionally credited with writing the Torah?', 'multiple_choice', '["Abraham", "David", "Moses", "Samuel"]', 'Moses', 6),
(690002, 'The book of Numbers gets its name from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Numerical symbolism", "Census counts of Israel", "Mathematical laws", "Numbered commandments"]', 'Census counts of Israel', 7),
(690002, 'Deuteronomy means:', 'multiple_choice', '["First law", "Second law", "Divine law", "Eternal law"]', 'Second law', 8),
(690002, 'The Abrahamic covenant includes the promise of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only spiritual blessings", "Land, descendants, and blessing", "Immediate salvation", "Political power"]', 'Land, descendants, and blessing', 9),
(690002, 'The Passover commemorates:', 'multiple_choice', '["The creation", "Israel''s deliverance from Egypt", "The giving of the Law", "The conquest of Canaan"]', 'Israel''s deliverance from Egypt', 10),
(690002, 'Describe the significance of the Mosaic covenant and how it shaped Israel''s identity as God''s chosen people.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The Mosaic covenant established Israel''s unique relationship with God, providing laws for worship, ethics, and community life that set them apart as a holy nation.', 11),

-- Lesson 3: Historical Books
(690003, 'The Historical Books span from which book to which book?', 'multiple_choice', '["Genesis to Deuteronomy", "Joshua to Esther", "Job to Song of Solomon", "Isaiah to Malachi"]', 'Joshua to Esther', 1),
(690003, 'Who led Israel into the Promised Land after Moses?', 'multiple_choice', '["Aaron", "Caleb", "Joshua", "Samuel"]', 'Joshua', 2),
(690003, 'The period of the Judges was characterized by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Strong central government", "Cycles of sin, oppression, and deliverance", "Peaceful prosperity", "Prophetic leadership"]', 'Cycles of sin, oppression, and deliverance', 3),
(690003, 'Who was Israel''s first king?', 'multiple_choice', '["David", "Solomon", "Saul", "Samuel"]', 'Saul', 4),
(690003, 'The united monarchy split into Israel and Judah after the reign of:', 'multiple_choice', '["David", "Solomon", "Rehoboam", "Jeroboam"]', 'Solomon', 5),
(690003, 'The Northern Kingdom (Israel) fell to which empire?', 'multiple_choice', '["Egypt", "Babylon", "Assyria", "Persia"]', 'Assyria', 6),
(690003, 'The Southern Kingdom (Judah) was conquered by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Egypt", "Babylon", "Assyria", "Persia"]', 'Babylon', 7),
(690003, 'Which Persian king allowed the Jews to return from exile?', 'multiple_choice', '["Nebuchadnezzar", "Cyrus", "Darius", "Xerxes"]', 'Cyrus', 8),
(690003, 'The book of Ruth is set during the time of:', 'multiple_choice', '["The patriarchs", "The judges", "The monarchy", "The exile"]', 'The judges', 9),
(690003, 'Nehemiah led the effort to rebuild:', 'multiple_choice', '["The temple", "The walls of Jerusalem", "The palace", "The tabernacle"]', 'The walls of Jerusalem', 10),
(690003, 'Explain the theological significance of the Davidic covenant and its connection to the Messiah.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The Davidic covenant promised an eternal throne to David''s descendants, ultimately fulfilled in Jesus Christ as the eternal King.', 11),

-- Lesson 4: Wisdom Literature
(690004, 'Which books comprise the Wisdom Literature?', 'multiple_choice', '["Genesis through Deuteronomy", "Joshua through Esther", "Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, Song of Solomon", "Isaiah through Malachi"]', 'Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, Song of Solomon', 1),
(690004, 'The book of Job primarily addresses:', 'multiple_choice', '["The history of Israel", "The problem of suffering", "Laws for daily living", "Future prophecies"]', 'The problem of suffering', 2),
(690004, 'How many Psalms are in the book of Psalms?', 'multiple_choice', '["100", "119", "150", "175"]', '150', 3),
(690004, 'Who is traditionally credited with writing most of Proverbs?', 'multiple_choice', '["David", "Solomon", "Moses", "Asaph"]', 'Solomon', 4),
(690004, 'The phrase "vanity of vanities" is found in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Job", "Psalms", "Proverbs", "Ecclesiastes"]', 'Ecclesiastes', 5),
(690004, 'The Song of Solomon is primarily about:', 'multiple_choice', '["Temple worship", "Love and marriage", "Wisdom sayings", "Prophetic visions"]', 'Love and marriage', 6),
(690004, 'In Hebrew wisdom literature, "the fear of the LORD" is described as:', 'multiple_choice', '["Terror of God", "The beginning of wisdom", "Unnecessary for the wise", "Only for priests"]', 'The beginning of wisdom', 7),
(690004, 'The Psalms were primarily used for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Private reading only", "Legal instruction", "Worship and prayer", "Historical records"]', 'Worship and prayer', 8),
(690004, 'Job''s friends argued that his suffering was due to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random chance", "His personal sin", "God''s absence", "Satan''s power"]', 'His personal sin', 9),
(690004, 'The conclusion of Ecclesiastes emphasizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Pursuing pleasure", "Gaining wealth", "Fearing God and keeping His commandments", "Avoiding all work"]', 'Fearing God and keeping His commandments', 10),
(690004, 'How does the book of Job challenge simplistic views of suffering and divine justice?', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Job demonstrates that suffering is not always punishment for sin and that God''s ways transcend human understanding.', 11),

-- Lesson 5: Major Prophets - Isaiah
(690005, 'Isaiah prophesied primarily during which period?', 'multiple_choice', '["The Exodus", "The Judges", "The divided monarchy", "The exile"]', 'The divided monarchy', 1),
(690005, 'Isaiah 53 is known as:', 'multiple_choice', '["The creation account", "The Suffering Servant passage", "The Ten Commandments", "The Beatitudes"]', 'The Suffering Servant passage', 2),
(690005, 'The virgin birth prophecy is found in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Isaiah 1:1", "Isaiah 7:14", "Isaiah 40:1", "Isaiah 66:1"]', 'Isaiah 7:14', 3),
(690005, 'Isaiah 40-66 is often called:', 'multiple_choice', '["The Book of Judgment", "The Book of Consolation", "The Book of Law", "The Book of History"]', 'The Book of Consolation', 4),
(690005, 'The name "Immanuel" means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Prince of Peace", "God with us", "Mighty God", "Everlasting Father"]', 'God with us', 5),
(690005, 'Isaiah''s call to ministry is recorded in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Isaiah 1", "Isaiah 6", "Isaiah 40", "Isaiah 53"]', 'Isaiah 6', 6),
(690005, 'The "Servant Songs" in Isaiah refer to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Temple musicians", "The Messiah", "The prophets", "The priests"]', 'The Messiah', 7),
(690005, 'Isaiah prophesied that Cyrus would:', 'multiple_choice', '["Destroy Jerusalem", "Allow Israel to return from exile", "Build the first temple", "Conquer Egypt"]', 'Allow Israel to return from exile', 8),
(690005, 'The phrase "Holy One of Israel" appears frequently in:', 'multiple_choice', '["Jeremiah", "Ezekiel", "Isaiah", "Daniel"]', 'Isaiah', 9),
(690005, 'Isaiah 9:6 describes the coming Messiah as:', 'multiple_choice', '["A suffering servant only", "Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace", "A political revolutionary", "A military conqueror"]', 'Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace', 10),
(690005, 'Explain how Isaiah 53 prophetically describes Jesus Christ and His atoning work.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'Isaiah 53 describes the Messiah bearing our sins, being wounded for our transgressions, and bringing healing through His suffering - fulfilled in Christ''s crucifixion.', 11);
command:
